projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

observations.summary: Roxanne and Steve are in the kitchen area, likely preparing breakfast or coffee. Simultaneously, the dogs are barking at the entryway and backyard, reacting to something or someone outside.

The dog run is currently empty, with only a single tennis ball visible on the artificial grass. Although audio indicates movement in the backyard and barking from the living room, no dogs or people are present in this area.
Light use at 0.56 gal/min. Daily total: 191.7 gal.
Steve and Roxanne are walking through the living room. A dog is barking at the entryway, coinciding with movement sounds detected in the living room and dog run.

The front door camera is non-functional and displaying an error message, preventing any visual monitoring of the entryway. While audio indicates a dog is barking at the entryway, the lack of video feed makes it impossible to see what is happening.
The side passage and backyard appear empty with the gate closed. However, audio indicates movement in the backyard and a dog barking, suggesting activity just out of view.
Light use at 1.24 gal/min. Daily total: 169.6 gal.
Penelope is moving around the kitchen area, visible in the background behind the living room couch. This movement aligns with the recent audio detection of movement in the living room.
The living room is currently empty and quiet. While audio indicates movement in the living room, no people or animals are visible in the frame, and the kitchen area appears unoccupied.

The front yard camera is intermittently failing, displaying a technical error message instead of a live feed in several frames. When the stream is active, the dark green pickup is parked in the driveway and Roxanne's black Cayenne is parked on the street.

insights.summary: [living_room] Dogs are barking at the entryway and backyard, which may indicate a visitor or activity near the house.
[living_room] Dog barking at entryway accompanied by movement sounds in the living room.
[doorbell] The front door camera is down, and audio indicates activity (dog barking) at the entryway that cannot be visually monitored.

This suggests potential external activity or an issue requiring attention, as the perimeter and dog monitoring are currently blind.
